title: docker and podman have different semantics around non existing host volumes
body:
/kind bug
**Description**

I don't know yet how this discrepancy will hit us in openstack, but since I noticed it, I thought it is at least worth reporting.

A) docker
```
$ ls -l /non-existant
ls: cannot access '/non-existant': No such file or directory
$ sudo docker run --name=test20 --net=host --user=root --rm -v /non-existant:/mnt/foo -it docker.io/centos /bin/sh -c 'mount|grep -i mnt; ls -lR /mnt'                                                        2 ↵
/dev/mapper/fedora_holtby-root on /mnt/foo type xfs (rw,relatime,seclabel,attr2,inode64,noquota)
/mnt:
total 0
drwxr-xr-x. 2 root root 6 Aug 22 18:54 foo

/mnt/foo:
total 0
```

I.e. somehow docker creates the non existing folder
```
$ ls -la /non-existant
total 0
drwxr-xr-x.  2 root root   6 Aug 22 20:54 .
dr-xr-xr-x. 18 root root 244 Aug 22 20:54 ..
```

B) podman
```
$ sudo rm -rf /non-existant
$ podman run --name=test20 --net=host --user=root --rm -v /non-existant:/mnt/foo -it docker.io/centos /bin/sh -c 'mount|grep -i mnt; ls -lR /mnt'
error checking path "/non-existant": stat /non-existant: no such file or directory
```

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:       0.8.3
Go Version:    go1.10.3
OS/Arch:       linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
host:
  Conmon:
    package: podman-0.8.3-4.git9d09a4d.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 1.12.0-dev, commit: 137533eb357b85f97b0dc9900326754c807901b6-dirty'
  MemFree: 1094594560
  MemTotal: 16690200576
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-46.dev.gitb4e2ecb.fc28.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: 'runc version spec: 1.0.0'
  SwapFree: 0
  SwapTotal: 0
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  hostname: holtby
  kernel: 4.18.1-300.fc29.x86_64
  os: linux
  uptime: 11h 6m 52.41s (Approximately 0.46 days)
insecure registries:
  registries: []
registries:
  registries:
  - docker.io
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - quay.io
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - registry.centos.org
store:
  ContainerStore:
    number: 10
  GraphDriverName: vfs
  GraphOptions: []
  GraphRoot: /home/michele/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus: {}
  ImageStore:
    number: 2
  RunRoot: /run/user/1000/run
```

comments.body: Do you think this is a bug in docker or in podman?  I am a little worried about creating a directory on disk because of a typo in a volume command.

For now, I just thought I'd mention this discrepancy as we noticed it while porting openstack from docker. We'll update things here as we make progress. Worst thing we could document this in the wiki page were we show the differences (which right now I can't find anymore)
